title: Step 5 followup: verify partial harness files + complete runner.ts
description: Step 5 (mpfr-ts-odi.5) was stopped mid-stream due to session-end. eval/harness/value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts, worker.ts exist; runner.ts does not. Next agent: (1) Read all three for senior-TS quality issues; (2) verify they align with the Step 5 brief (esp. value_codec ExpectedOutput shape, worker keep-alive pattern, ast_check Cyrillic detection); (3) write runner.ts per spec; (4) execute the full acceptance suite (stub correct port pass, broken port fail, infloop terminates fast, schema violation rejected, NaN equality OK). Only close mpfr-ts-odi.5 after acceptance is GREEN.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 10: Live sonnet L3 attempt + finalize
description: Render the mpn_add_n prompt via python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n. Save to /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/prompt.txt. Orchestrator (parent Claude session) spawns ONE Agent(subagent_type=general-purpose, model=sonnet) with the prompt as task; agent writes /tmp/eval_pilot_mpn_add_n/port.ts. After agent returns, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts to grade; ast_check the port for core.ts import compliance; INSERT into runs table; UPDATE functions row. Accept: composite_correctness ≥ 0.95 AND ast_check passes AND state.db row reflects the run. If composite < 0.95 → file bd bug describing failure mode (Cyrillic? schema-violation? wrong carry? infinite loop?), HALT pilot per PIL.1. If composite ≥ 0.95 → write docs/worklog/001-pilot-mpn-add-n.md retrospective. PIL.5 is met only when ALL 10 pilot fns reach this state — this is just the first.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 9: Prompt template + dry-run driver
description: Create eval/driver/prompts.py: build_prompt(run_info) emits the L3 sonnet prompt — embeds src/core.ts verbatim (Law 4 enforcement), embeds the C source from mpfr/src/ (or GMP equivalent for mpn fns), embeds spec.json contents, embeds the iteration loop instructions (write port to <path>, run bun eval/harness/runner.ts --function X --port <path> --golden <path> --output <path>, read grade.json, iterate up to 6 times, stop when composite ≥ 0.95). Create eval/driver/ralph.py with --dry-run flag that prints the rendered prompt without spawning an Agent, --function flag to pick which function. Accept: python3 eval/driver/ralph.py --function mpn_add_n --dry-run prints a complete prompt containing the words 'MPFR' (from core.ts), 'mpn_add_n' (from spec), the C signature, and the iteration loop instructions; prompt size between 5KB and 50KB.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 8: Mutation-prove the golden
description: Run bun eval/harness/runner.ts twice: once against correct/mpn_add_n.ts, once against broken/mpn_add_n.ts. Record grade.json from both. Accept: correct composite_correctness ≥ 0.99 (ideally 1.0); broken composite_correctness ≤ 0.5; gap ≥ 0.49. If correct < 0.99 → the golden is wrong (or the reference port is wrong) — debug before continuing. If broken > 0.5 → the golden is insufficient (not catching the seeded bug) — add adversarial cases until it does. This is THE harness-soundness gate of the pilot.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 7: Reference ports (correct + broken)
description: Create eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (hand-written, faithful, well-cited per Law 1) and eval/reference_ports/broken/mpn_add_n.ts (deliberately wrong: skip the carry propagation past index 0, or return carry as always 0n). NOTE: mpn_add_n is a substrate fn — it does NOT use the MPFR value type, so the core.ts import requirement doesn't apply. The spec is bigint[] in, {sum:bigint[], carry:bigint} out. Cite GMP §8.3 for limb endianness. Both files should be runnable via: bun eval/reference_ports/correct/mpn_add_n.ts (with a 3-line smoke import at the bottom guarded by import.meta.main). Accept: both files type-check, both export mpn_add_n with the spec signature, correct version handles single-limb and multi-limb correctly when tested by hand at REPL.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 6: mpn_add_n spec + golden_driver + golden.jsonl
description: Create eval/functions/mpn_add_n/{spec.json, golden_driver.c}. spec.json: name=mpn_add_n, class=substrate, c_signature='mp_limb_t mpn_add_n(mp_limb_t *rp, const mp_limb_t *s1p, const mp_limb_t *s2p, mp_size_t n)', ts_signature='export function mpn_add_n(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number): {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps=[], notes (limb endianness, carry semantics, n>=1 invariant). golden_driver.c: includes common.h, generates ~180 cases — happy (n=1..5, random limbs, 30 cases), edge (n=1 zero, all-ones max carry chain, single high bit, MSB of each limb, n=20 cascading carry, 40 cases), adversarial (n=50 all-0xff to force max carry, n=10 with carry-out at boundary, 20 cases), fuzz (deterministic xorshift, n in {1,2,3,5,10,20,50}, 90 cases). Each case emits {tag, inputs:{a:["123",...], b:[...], n:5}, output:{sum:[...], carry:"1"}, time_ns}. Accept: bash eval/golden_master/build.sh succeeds; bash run produces golden.jsonl with wc -l ≥ 150; all 4 tags present (grep -c '"tag":"happy"' ≥ 20, edge ≥ 30, adversarial ≥ 10, fuzz ≥ 50).
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 5: Harness skeleton (Bun worker pool)
description: Create eval/harness/{runner.ts, worker.ts, value_codec.ts, ast_check.ts} using Bun Worker API. runner.ts: parse args (--function, --port, --golden, --output), spawn worker pool of N=4, dispatch one case per message, class-tier hard wall (substrate=50ms, arithmetic=50ms, transcendental=200ms, misc=1s), worker.terminate() on timeout; aggregate composite per CLAUDE.md formula (0.6 corr + 0.2 edge + 0.2 mined; perf grade SEPARATE; composite=0 if compiled=false). worker.ts: imports port via dynamic import, calls fn with parsed args, returns {result, ms}. value_codec.ts: decodeMpfrValue(obj)->MPFR per src/core.ts; encodeForCompare(MPFR)->canonical JSON. ast_check.ts: parse port.ts string; verify 'import {...MPFR...} from "...core.ts"' present; verify no 'interface MPFR' or 'type RoundingMode' declarations; return {ok, errors}. Accept: smoke test against a stub port + stub golden (10 cases, all should pass) produces grade.json with composite=1.0; against a stub port with one wrong return produces composite<1.
notes: PARTIAL — agent stopped mid-step. Files written: eval/harness/{ast_check.ts (9KB), value_codec.ts (19KB), worker.ts (6KB)}. Files NOT written: eval/harness/runner.ts. Acceptance tests NOT run. Next session must (a) inspect the three written files for senior-TS quality, (b) write runner.ts per Step 5 brief, (c) run the FULL acceptance suite from the brief — especially the infinite-loop test. The brief is preserved as bd issue mpfr-ts-odi.5 description; re-read in full before continuing.
close_reason: Pilot completed in worklog 003 (Apr/May session); 115 ports landed since. All work in these tickets is done.

title: Step 4: Golden-driver common.h
description: Create eval/golden_master/common.h adapted from ../auto-port-eval/eval/golden_master/common.h: keep xs64_seed/xs64_next/xs64_below PRNG, now_ns, jl_begin/jl_end_inputs/jl_finish JSONL helpers. ADD: jl_limb_array(f, key, limbs, n) — emits '"key":["123","456",...]' for mpn arrays (decimal strings to keep BigInt parse trivial). ADD: jl_mpfr_value(f, key, x) — emits MPFR value as JSON object (kind, sign, prec, exp_str, mant_hex via mpfr_get_str base 16). Include gmp.h and mpfr.h. Accept: 10-line test driver compiles + emits one valid JSON line that round-trips through Bun's JSON.parse.

title: Step 3: State DB schema + init
description: Create eval/driver/schema.sql with the three tables from CLAUDE.md §'State DB schema': functions, runs, cases (verbatim). Apply via sqlite3 eval/state.db < eval/driver/schema.sql. Insert one row: functions.name='mpn_add_n', class='substrate', signature='(a: bigint[], b: bigint[], n: number) => {sum: bigint[], carry: bigint}', deps='[]', status='pending', topo_rank=0. Accept: sqlite3 eval/state.db 'SELECT name, class, status FROM functions' shows mpn_add_n|substrate|pending.

title: Step 2: Locked schema src/core.ts
description: Implement src/core.ts EXACTLY as specified in CLAUDE.md §'Library coherence': MPFR interface (kind, sign, prec, exp, mant), RoundingMode string union ('RNDN'|'RNDZ'|'RNDU'|'RNDD'|'RNDA'), Ternary type (-1|0|1), Result interface ({value, ternary}), MPFRError class with code 'EPREC'|'EROUND'|'EDOMAIN'. No Bun.* or node:* imports. Accept: bun -e "import('./src/core.ts').then(m=>console.log(Object.keys(m)))" lists MPFRError; tsc --noEmit src/core.ts passes.

title: Step 1: Repo skeleton + system deps
description: Create PHASE.md, package.json (engines: bun>=1.3, node>=22), bunfig.toml, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, dir tree (src/, src/internal/{mpn,mpfr}, eval/{functions,harness,golden_master,driver,reference_ports/{correct,broken},integration,reports}, docs/{worklog,adr}). Verify libmpfr-dev, libgmp-dev, gcc, sqlite3, python3+venv, bun>=1.3, node>=22. Clone https://gitlab.inria.fr/mpfr/mpfr.git depth=1 to ./mpfr/. Accept: bun --version, gcc --version, pkg-config --cflags mpfr, ls mpfr/src/add.c all succeed.

title: Pilot: vertical slice for mpn_add_n
description: Epic tracking the 10 steps of the Pilot phase. Deliverable: harness proven sound by grading mpn_add_n end-to-end. See docs/PILOT_PLAN.md.

title: validate_specs.py: structural diff of gen_spec output vs curated spec.json across all functions
description:
Step 2 of pre-trial validation. Build eval/driver/validate_specs.py to run gen_spec.extract_spec against every function we have a curated spec.json for, and emit a structural diff report.

Public API:
- validate_spec(c_source_path, function, curated_spec_path) -> SpecValidationResult
- validate_all_specs(eval_functions_dir, mpfr_src_dir, callgraph_path) -> list[SpecValidationResult]
- main() CLI

Dataclasses (frozen=True):
- SpecFieldDiff(field: str, gen_spec_value, curated_value, matches: bool)
- SpecValidationResult(function: str, c_source: Path, curated_spec: Path, diffs: list[SpecFieldDiff], extraction_error: str | None)

Fields to diff structurally (not prose):
- class (str equality)
- signature.params (list equality, order matters)
- signature.returns (str equality)
- c_signature (str equality with whitespace normalization on both sides)
- prec_unit (str equality)

Fields to SKIP (intentionally differ): function (always input), doc, divergence_from_c, refs.

CLI behavior:
- python3 eval/driver/validate_specs.py [--json | --summary | --function FN]
- --summary (default): pretty table per-function, plus aggregate stats (N functions checked, N matched, N with diffs, N with extraction errors)
- --json: machine-readable per-function results
- --function FN: only check one function (useful for debugging)

Mapping function -> C source: read eval/driver/callgraph.json's 'functions' object, lookup defined_in field, prepend mpfr/src/.

Acceptance:
- TDD with pytest cases for the dataclasses + diff logic + a smoke test against 2-3 real fixtures (mpfr_add_d, mpfr_cmp, mpfr_init2)
- Handles gracefully: extract_spec raises, callgraph missing entry, C source file missing
- 100-150 LOC for validate_specs.py + 60-90 LOC tests
- pytest passes
close_reason: validate_specs.py landed: 167 LOC tool + 108 LOC tests (7 pytest cases, all pass). CLI works: --function add_d --summary shows class/c_signature diffs; --function cmp --summary shows class/params/c_signature/prec_unit diffs. Surfaced new findings on first 2 fixtures: (1) curated 'class' field tracks runtime budget tier (misc=1000ms) not semantic family, so gen_spec's filename heuristic systematically disagrees; (2) curated params use semantic names (a/b) where gen_spec uses literal C decl names (b/c); (3) some curated specs are missing prec_unit. Analysis of full 119-function corpus is step 3.

title: Diverse import-shape regression tests for _rewrite_relative_imports
description:
Step 1 of pre-trial validation plan. Currently _rewrite_relative_imports in eval/driver/mutate.py is tested implicitly by the calibration tests (which only exercise simple 'import { ... } from "../core.ts"' shapes). Before relying on it across the full codebase of 115 ports, harden it with explicit regression tests covering the import shapes that actually appear in the codebase.

Shapes to cover:
1. Multi-line imports: 'import {\n  mpfr_add,\n  mpfr_mul,\n} from "../ops/index.ts";'
2. Re-exports: 'export { mpfr_add } from "../ops/add.ts";'
3. Type-only imports: 'import type { MPFR } from "../core.ts";'
4. Mixed type imports: 'import { type MPFR, RoundingMode } from "../core.ts";'
5. Side-effect imports (must NOT be rewritten if no from clause): 'import "./polyfill.ts";' (defensive — these would also need rewriting but might not be in mpfr-ts)
6. Bare-package imports (must NOT be touched): 'import { x } from "some-package";'
7. Relative non-core: 'from "../internal/mpn/add_n.ts"'

Acceptance:
- 5-8 new pytest cases added to eval/driver/tests/test_mutate.py
- Each case uses a helper that calls mutate._rewrite_relative_imports directly with a synthetic source string
- TDD: red first (any case where the function fails to handle the shape), then green if a fix is needed; if all current shapes already work, document that and add the tests as regression coverage
- LOC delta: +30 to +50 LOC in test_mutate.py (currently 145; cap 200)
- All existing 6 tests in test_mutate.py continue to pass
close_reason: Added 8 import-shape regression tests covering: multi-line imports, re-exports, type-only imports, mixed type imports, sibling-relative paths, cross-tree relatives, double-quoted variants, bare-package non-rewrite. All pass without any mutate.py change — existing regex (?<=from )(['\"])(\.\.?/[^'\"]+)\1 is already robust because every shape's path follows the literal 'from ' keyword. +39 LOC delta to test_mutate.py (145 -> 184). All 14 tests pass (8 new + 6 existing including 2 slow calibration runs).

title: Strengthen mutator menu: current 4-mutation menu clean-kills only 1/6 across calibration fixtures
description:
The mutate.py calibration tests (mpfr_add_d, mpfr_sqr_2) revealed that the current 4-mutation menu in eval/driver/mutators.ts is structurally too weak to replace opus's hand-picked broken-port deliverable.

Observed calibration after import-rewrite fix (2026-05-24):

mpfr_add_d (3 applicable mutations):
  op-swap        composite=0.0000  clean kill ✓
  rnd-swap       composite=1.0000  NOT killed — golden RNDN/RNDZ cases produce identical results often
  ternary-negate composite=1.0000  NOT killed — most golden cases have ternary=0; negating 0 = 0

mpfr_sqr_2 (3 applicable mutations):
  rnd-swap       composite=0.8930  weak kill (below 0.95 but not <0.55)
  ternary-negate composite=1.0000  NOT killed — same exact-result issue
  sign-flip      composite=1.0000  NOT killed — sign-flip only touches NaN/inf/zero special-case branches

1/6 clean kills total. The original risk-mitigation target was 4/6 (set in mutate.py prompt). Current menu cannot serve as a drop-in replacement for opus's hand-designed perturbation.

PROPOSED MENU EXTENSIONS:
1. constant-bump: find <int>n bigint literals in non-import contexts (e.g., 53n, 63n, 192n) and bump by ±1. Catches off-by-one prec/shift bugs.
2. comparison-swap: '<' ↔ '<=' and '>' ↔ '>=' in conditional contexts. Catches boundary errors.
3. shift-direction-swap: '>>' ↔ '<<' (BigInt shifts particularly). Catches direction-confusion bugs.
4. arith-binop-swap: '+' ↔ '-' in non-call positions (e.g., 'a + b' but not 'mpfr_add(...)'). Catches sign errors in inline arithmetic.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- constant-bump is the most likely to land clean kills broadly (touches the exponent/sticky-bit math that ternary-negate misses)
- comparison-swap requires careful AST-context detection (regex on conditions only, not assignments)
- shift-direction-swap is high-value for substrate ports (mpn_* functions are shift-heavy)
- arith-binop-swap risks matching '+' in string concatenation; needs constraint to numeric contexts

ACCEPTANCE: re-running mutate.py calibration tests on mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2 + mpfr_cmp + mpfr_div_1 (4 fixtures) should show ≥3 clean kills total (vs current 1 across 2 fixtures).

LOC budget: ~80 LOC added to mutators.ts (currently 101) + ~50 LOC of new tests. Total ~150 LOC delta.

This is the follow-up promised in the step 3 commit message and worklog 008. Not blocking the broader automation effort — mutate.py is structurally sound and lands gate_passed=True for ports where op-swap applies. Strengthens once this lands.
close_reason: Menu extended with 3 new mutations: bigint-bump (single-target bump of first bigint literal >=2), comparison-swap (swap <,<=,>,>= in first runtime occurrence with generics-guard), shift-direction-swap (bidirectional >>/<<). Plus maskComments helper (caught during calibration: bigint-bump was matching JSDoc literals). Real calibration: 3 clean kills across mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2 (vs prior 1, target was 3). shift-direction-swap landed composite=0.0 on sqr_2; bigint-bump landed 0.528 on sqr_2. comparison-swap weak on both fixtures (hits prec-validation guards not exercised in normal-path tests) — worth a P4 follow-up but not blocking.

title: mutate.py: Python orchestrator that runs mutators + grader, validates calibration
description:
Build eval/driver/mutate.py: Python orchestrator that drives mutators.ts + runner.ts to mutation-prove a port.

Public API:
- mutation_prove(function_name, port_path, golden_path, tmp_dir=None) -> ProveResult

ProveResult dataclass:
- function_name: str
- mutations: list[MutationResult]  -- per applicable mutation
- gate_passed: bool                -- at least 1 mutation scored <= 0.95 (CLAUDE.md PIL.3 threshold)
- clean_kills: int                 -- count of mutations that scored <= 0.55 (worklog 006 #6 standard)

MutationResult dataclass:
- name: str
- composite: float | None    -- None if mutator returned applied=false
- below_threshold: bool      -- composite <= 0.95
- clean_kill: bool           -- composite <= 0.55

CLI:
- python3 eval/driver/mutate.py --function <fn> --port <path> --golden <path> [--tmp-dir <path>]
  Prints a summary table and exits 0 if gate_passed, 1 otherwise.

Implementation:
1. subprocess: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts list --input <port>' -> list of applicable mutations
2. For each: produce /tmp/mutant_<fn>_<mut>.ts via mutators.ts apply
3. subprocess: 'bun eval/harness/runner.ts --function <fn> --port <mutant> --golden <golden> --output /tmp/grade_<fn>_<mut>.json'
4. Parse grade.json, extract composite_correctness
5. Aggregate + return

Calibration target (the actual gate this is meant to enforce):
- For mpfr_add_d (3 applicable mutations): ALL applied mutations must score <= 0.95; >=2 must score <= 0.55
- For mpfr_sqr_2 (3 applicable mutations): same

Tests live in eval/driver/tests/test_mutate.py. Calibration tests against the two real fixtures above run as part of pytest (slower but mandatory).

Acceptance:
- TDD: red first, then green
- pytest passes against both fixtures
- 150-200 LOC total (orchestrator + tests)
- subprocess timeouts of 30s per grader call (prevent infinite loops from a bad mutant)
- Cleanup: mutate.py removes /tmp/mutant_*.ts and /tmp/grade_*.json on exit

Risks the subagent must guard against:
- Bad mutant infinite-loops in the grader -- subprocess timeout
- grade.json malformed -- catch JSONDecodeError, treat as composite=None
- Mutator exits with code 3 (not applicable) when listApplicableMutations said it applies -- treat as test failure (calibration drift)
- mpfr-ts-6s9: bunfig.toml preload=[] is cwd-sensitive -- invoke bun from repo root explicitly
close_reason: mutate.py landed: 242 LOC orchestrator + 140 LOC tests (6 pytest cases, all pass). Import-rewrite + module_init_failed detection both functional. Calibration findings: current menu lands 1/6 clean kills across mpfr_add_d + mpfr_sqr_2; menu-strengthening filed as follow-up bd.

title: mutators.ts: regex-based AST mutator engine for mutation-proving
description:
Build eval/driver/mutators.ts: a Bun-native regex-based mutator that applies one of several deterministic perturbations to a TS port file.

Public API:
- applyMutation(source: string, mutation: MutationName): { mutated: string; applied: boolean; description: string }
- listApplicableMutations(source: string): MutationName[]
- CLI: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts apply --input <path> --output <path> --mutation <name>'
- CLI: 'bun eval/driver/mutators.ts list --input <path>' -- prints applicable mutations

Mutation menu (4 to start):
1. op-swap: swap ALL occurrences of mpfr_add(/mpfr_sub( pairs and mpfr_mul(/mpfr_div( pairs (whichever appears first in the file)
2. rnd-swap: swap ALL occurrences of 'RNDN'/'RNDZ' or 'RNDU'/'RNDD' (string-literal form only)
3. ternary-negate: find 'ternary: <ident-or-literal>' in return positions and negate the value
4. sign-flip: swap 'sign: 1' with 'sign: -1' (and the reverse if present)

Each mutation returns applied=false (and unchanged source) if no matching pattern is present.

Style: regex-based, matches eval/harness/ast_check.ts convention. No AST library deps.

Acceptance:
- TDD: red test first, then green
- Tests cover each mutation applied + each mutation no-op case
- Tests assert applied mutants differ from source AND parse cleanly (round-trip via Bun.Transpiler or similar)
- Cyrillic-safe: do not introduce non-ASCII chars
- Comments/strings: brief check that the regex doesn't accidentally match inside line comments or string literals containing the trigger token
- 150-200 LOC total (mutator + tests)
- 'bun test eval/driver/mutators.test.ts' passes
close_reason: mutators.ts landed: 101 LOC + 99 LOC tests (12 bun:test cases, all pass via bun test from eval/driver/). 4 mutations (op-swap, rnd-swap, ternary-negate, sign-flip) all confirmed via real-source smoke test on add_d.ts and sqr_2.ts. Round-trip transpile via Bun.Transpiler verifies syntactic validity of every applied mutant. Subagent caught + fixed a real bug mid-implementation: initial ternary-negate regex matched 'const ternary: Ternary = ...;' declarations and broke sqr_2 transpile; tightened to require leading { or , for object-literal context.

title: gen_spec.py: extract structural spec fields from C source
description:
Build eval/driver/gen_spec.py with extract_spec(c_source_path, function_name, class_hint=None) -> dict.

Outputs a partial spec.json dict with these structural fields populated:
- function (str): the function name
- class (str): one of {substrate, arithmetic, transcendental, misc} — inferred from C source path (mpn -> substrate; add*.c/sub*.c/mul*.c/div*.c/sqr*.c/sqrt*.c -> arithmetic; exp*.c/log*.c/sin*.c/cos*.c/tan*.c/asin*.c/acos*.c/atan*.c/sinh*.c/cosh*.c/tanh*.c -> transcendental; else misc). Class hint overrides.
- signature.params (list[str]): TS param names derived from C param names, dropping the output slot (mpfr_ptr first arg)
- signature.returns (str): 'Result' if last C param is mpfr_rnd_t and first is mpfr_ptr; 'number' if C returns int and no mpfr_ptr; etc.
- c_signature (str): the exact C signature line, normalized to single line
- prec_unit (str): always 'bits'
- doc (str): 'TODO: opus fills this in'
- divergence_from_c (str): 'TODO: opus fills this in'
- refs (list[str]): templated minimum: mpfr/src/<fn>.c, src/core.ts, CLAUDE.md hallucination callouts

Acceptance:
- TDD: red test first (shows failing), then green (shows passing)
- Tests cover: mpfr_add_d (simple wrapper), mpfr_sqr_2 (single-limb fast path), mpfr_cmp (no rop, returns int), mpfr_get_str (string output, complex sig), mpfr_init2 (substrate-style init)
- Unknown C types emit 'TODO: <type>' not a guess
- 150-200 LOC total including tests
- pytest eval/driver/tests/test_gen_spec.py passes
close_reason: gen_spec.py landed: 152 LOC module + 111 LOC tests (8 pytest cases, all pass). Real-source smoke-tested against mpfr/src/add_d.c, cmp.c, init2.c, get_str.c — handles multi-line decls, static-skipping, unknown-type TODOs correctly.

title: Run `bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl` before every git commit
description: The mpfr-ts-NEW1/2r8/akk/etc engine issues from the previous-session HANDOFF were never persisted to git because no `bd export` ran before the commits. Local dolt had them; JSONL did not; new device pulled and lost them. The HANDOFF.md gotcha #2 documents this trap. Either: pre-commit hook that does the export, or a wrapper around bd-mutating commands. Until then, all bd changes need a manual export step before commit.

title: callgraph.py misses mpn_* substrate fns (sourced from GMP)
description: callgraph.py walks mpfr/src/*.c only; mpn_* helpers live in the GMP source tree, not MPFR. They're referenced by deps lists but never appear as candidate fns. Means --next can never schedule a missing mpn_* port. Fix: also walk gmp/mpn/generic/*.c (or stub mpn_* entries). Old issue mpfr-ts-9li.

title: value_codec scalar-string outputs
description: Some MPFR functions return strings (mpfr_get_str eventually). Wire codec only handles bigint/number/object outputs. Add string scalar handling. Old issue mpfr-ts-i5z.

title: Enforce Rule 7 tag minimums at grade time
description: CLAUDE.md Rule 7 says goldens must have happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5. Currently no automatic enforcement — agents can ship low-coverage goldens and they'll pass. Grader should reject (or warn) when class counts fall short. Old issue mpfr-ts-7em.

title: runner n_throw conflates exceptions with value mismatches
description: The runner reports n_throw for any non-pass case, including value mismatches that did NOT throw. Hit this session investigating mpn_cmp_aux failures: report said n_throw=11 but cases were returning wrong VALUES, not throwing. Misleading for diagnosis. Should split: n_throw (actual exceptions) vs n_mismatch (value disagreement). Old issue mpfr-ts-3ka.

title: expected_throw codec for goldens with domain errors
description: Functions like mpfr_abort_prec_max are blocked because the harness has no way to encode 'this input should cause the port to throw'. Currently the only output codec emits a value. Adds: a wire-level 'expected_throw' field to golden entries that the grader matches against caught exceptions from the port. Old issue mpfr-ts-79u.

title: Add eval/golden_master/run_all.sh wrapper
description: build.sh compiles drivers; there's no scripted equivalent for running them to produce golden.jsonl. Currently the orchestrator runs per-driver: `./golden_driver > golden.jsonl` in a bash loop. A run_all.sh that iterates eval/functions/*/ would mirror build.sh and let the HANDOFF's documented `bash eval/golden_master/run_all.sh` actually work. Old issue mpfr-ts-0t3 (lost in dolt sync gap).

title: Bug averted: bd-init .gitignore '*.db' glob would have excluded eval/state.db
description: During Step 1 (mpfr-ts-odi.1) the subagent noticed bd init's default .gitignore contained '*.db' which would have silently excluded eval/state.db — violating CLAUDE.md Rule 9 (state.db is the single source of truth and MUST be checked in). Fix: replaced glob with explicit ignores and a sentinel comment. Filing as closed for the historical record so future agents debugging 'why is state.db not in git' can find this.

title: mine_tests.py: skip — MPFR test files use variable-fed inputs, not lift-able literals
description:
Investigated as part of the opus-prep automation effort (step 2 of 5). Conclusion: skip.

MPFR test files (e.g. mpfr/tests/tadd_d.c) overwhelmingly use variable-fed inputs at call sites: 'mpfr_add_d (y, x, d, MPFR_RNDN)' where y/x/d are constructed via earlier mpfr_set_* calls. Naive literal-arg extraction yields 0-3 usable cases per function (just the rounding modes and inline scalars).

A full dataflow miner that tracks mpfr_set_* assignments back to feeding literals is 300+ LOC, well over the original 100-150 LOC budget, and has silent-failure modes (incorrect lifts produce 'mined' cases that don't match libmpfr).

Per-function leverage is ~3 min of opus work — the smallest item in the original automation ranking. Cutting it saves ~10 hours across remaining functions, vs ~70 hours each from the mutator and driver-scaffold steps. Opus is genuinely good at the cognitive part of mining (picking representative shapes from a test file by reading prose context).

Decision: defer mining automation indefinitely. Reconsider only if a less-fragile approach surfaces (e.g. parsing mpfr's existing test-data JSON files, if they exist for any subset of functions).

title: state.db topo_rank diverges from callgraph topo_rank
description: Seeded rows store topo_rank from the callgraph at seed time. If callgraph.py is re-run (e.g. after pulling new mpfr/), ranks can shift but seeded rows keep old values. Picker uses state.db rank, which may now be inconsistent. Either: re-seed all rows on callgraph regen, or always read topo_rank live from callgraph.json. Old issue mpfr-ts-0dw.
